steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Before the blank is the independent clause "...are regarded as important events in US civil rights ______." After the blank is a participial phrase — "the former establishing a Latino rights advocacy group and the latter legally affirming..." — that elaborates on the two events. The blank must connect them correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "history, as the": inserts "as the" before "former," turning the participial phrase into a dependent clause. That clause needs a full verb ("as the former established..."), but "establishing" is a participle — the result is ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice B — "history. The": creates a period, making the participial phrase that follows ("The former establishing...") a sentence fragment with no finite verb. ✗
  • Choice C — "history the": no punctuation at all before "the former," fusing the clause and the elaborating phrase without any separator, which is a run-on. ✗
  • Choice D — "history, the": a comma correctly introduces the absolute/participial phrase "the former establishing... and the latter legally affirming..." as an elaboration of "civil rights history." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
